article_title: Linux 7.0 Released With Stable Rust Support, Self-Healing XFS, and sched_ext as Torvalds Reflects on AI-Driven Bug Discovery

content_preview.title: Linux 7.0 Released With Stable Rust Support, Self-Healing XFS, and sched_ext as Torvalds Reflects on AI-Driven Bug Discovery
content_preview.summary: Linux 7.0 arrives on April 12, graduating Rust from experimental status, introducing an extensible eBPF scheduler framework, and adding autonomous XFS repair capabilities.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Linus Torvalds released Linux 7.0 on April 12, 2026, closing a development cycle that began with the merge window opening on February 9. The first major version number change since Linux 6.0 in October 2022, the release delivers stable Rust language support in the kernel, an extensible scheduler framework built on eBPF, autonomous XFS filesystem repair, and hardware enablement for Intel and AMD next-generation processors, as [reported by The Register](https://www.theregister.com/202...
